name: SVG Animation (SMIL + CSS)
performance_characteristics: Resolution-independent vector animation; CSS-driven transform/opacity is compositor-friendly, while SMIL and path/attribute animation run on the main thread
unsuitable_contexts: very large or highly complex scenes (canvas/WebGL scale better)
strengths: Crisp, resolution-independent animation of icons, logos and illustrations
Can animate stroke, path morphing and attributes not reachable by plain CSS
CSS animation of SVG transform/opacity is GPU-friendly and accessible
Self-contained, scriptable and stylable within the DOM
weaknesses: SMIL has been historically deprecation-threatened and is uneven across engines; CSS/Web Animations are the safer path
Animating many nodes or complex filters is CPU-heavy and can jank
Accessibility requires explicit roles/labels and reduced-motion handling
